commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Discord quick-setup step telling package, npm, and Docker users to install `@openclaw/discord` before gateway startup and points configured-but-missing setups to `openclaw doctor --fix` or explicit install.

Reproducibility: Source-level yes, live no: current docs jump from config to `openclaw gateway`, while the package and catalog show Discord is external and installed as `@openclaw/discord`. I did not run a Docker/npm live setup or render the docs page.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

commands.review_summary:
The PR extends CLI output usage parsing to accept OpenAI-compatible `prompt_tokens`/`completion_tokens` aliases and adds a JSONL regression test for the llama.cpp-shaped result.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is clear: current `toCliUsage()` ignores `prompt_tokens` and `completion_tokens`, and `parseCliJsonl()` passes llama.cpp-style result usage through that helper. I did not run a live llama.cpp session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human/contributor action is needed for live proof or an explicit maintainer proof override, so this should not enter the repair lane.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds first-progress and startup-failed subagent lifecycle signals, propagates them through registry, gateway, and session wait paths, changes announce cleanup ordering, and updates tests, docs, and changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main emits and consumes only start/end/error in the relevant paths, while the PR adds a regression for start -> startup-failed -> trailing end. I did not establish a live failing run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed because the external PR still needs real behavior proof from an observed aborted subagent startup before merge.
